title: [codex] fix: repair auth modal theme and focus
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds the dialog theme boundary to the positioner so auth backgrounds resolve to amber.
- Replaces the logo mask shorthand with explicit quoted mask properties so the built SVG data URL renders correctly.
- Suppresses default browser focus outlines on the dialog/logo containers without changing tab order or control focus.
- Root cause: themed classes were resolving outside `data-theme`, and the inlined SVG mask URL could fail CSS parsing.
- Verified with `biome`, `@pollinations/ui` typecheck/build, Enter typecheck, and local auth/key-dialog checks.

gist.headline: A fresh coat of amber
gist.blurb: We've polished up the authentication modal! The amber theme now renders perfectly, the logo is crisp, and those pesky default focus outlines are gone.
gist.summary: Fixed CSS parsing and theme boundary issues in the auth modal to ensure the amber background and SVG logo mask render correctly.
gist.impact: Users will see a cleaner, properly themed authentication dialog without broken logos or default browser focus rings.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a painter or window-washer, polishing up a glowing amber gateway or login screen. The robot companion helps out. Represents UI polish, fixing visual bugs, and the specific amber theme mentioned in the PR.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny painter's beret, carefully polishing a glowing amber-colored digital gateway. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holds a bucket of lime green (#ecf874) paint nearby. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites, nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
